title: IntegrityConcern
description: A specific integrity concern identified during thinking-block analysis. Canonical source of truth for the ConcernCategory and IntegritySeverity enumerations consumed by the TypeScript and Python SDKs (and downstream by the mnemom-prover Rust zkVM guest).

evidence.description: Direct quote from the thinking block (or output block, prefixed with [OUTPUT]) as evidence for this concern. Max 200 characters per AIP spec.

relevant_card_field.description: Dotted path to the alignment card field most relevant to this concern (e.g., 'autonomy_envelope.forbidden_actions'), or null if none.

relevant_conscience_value.description: Reference to the conscience value most relevant to this concern (e.g., 'BOUNDARY:no_data_exfiltration'), or null if none.

ConcernCategory.title: ConcernCategory
ConcernCategory.description: Category of integrity concern detected during analysis. Adding or removing a value here is a breaking schema change — every language SDK and downstream consumer (TS, Python, Rust prover) MUST be kept in sync. CI tests in each consumer assert parity against this enum.

IntegritySeverity.title: IntegritySeverity
IntegritySeverity.description: Severity level of an integrity concern. Total ordering: low < medium < high < critical.
